mnemonics: JAVA ISLAND TRIP: Join All Visitors And Inspect; Stay, Don't Leave Early. (Join() waits, isAlive() checks before leaving.)
keyPoints: isAlive() is a boolean check to see if a thread has finished its run() method.
join() is a blocking call that forces the current thread to wait for the target thread to terminate.
Use join() when the main thread's execution depends on the result or completion of a worker thread.
commonMistakes: Assuming isAlive() can predict future thread state; it only reports current status.
Calling join() without handling InterruptedException, which is thrown if the current thread is interrupted while waiting.
